description: Discovery work in Arrive tests whether the combined parking, transit, partner, and planning portfolio can become a coherent platform rather than a loose set of acquired products.

riskFramework.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
Can customers figure out how to use it?
Can we build it within given resources and constraints?

items.name: City intelligence value model
items.summary: Discovery is testing whether city buyers value a combined planning-and-operations proposition strongly enough to expand beyond parking procurement into broader policy and analytics contracts.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether city mobility intelligence and planning products create enough cross-solution expansion to justify deeper platform investment.

linkedInitiatives.description: City intelligence operating model initiative focused on improving city mobility intelligence, planning, scenario & outcome modeling, operational data, KPI & analytics platform, and enterprise integration layer across City Mobility Insight Board and Mobility Intelligence Data API.

assignments.how: Leads buyer interviews, opportunity framing, and product-value synthesis for city intelligence.
Supports the discovery with intervention-modeling concepts and policy-outcome framing.
Supports the discovery with KPI-pack design and trusted reporting constraints.
Supports the discovery with municipal-ingestion feasibility and reuse assumptions.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: City buyers will pay more if planning insight clearly improves operational decisions rather than living as a dashboard layer beside them.
evidenceToCollect: Whether city buyers describe cross-domain mobility evidence as procurement-worthy rather than nice to have.
Which policy decisions would meaningfully change if shared planning data were available.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The business case works if planning subscriptions accelerate expansion inside parking and transport accounts rather than requiring a separate sales motion.
evidenceToCollect: What attach and expansion rates make the planning product viable.
Whether city-planning work materially improves retention of operational accounts.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: City users can interpret outcome modeling only if operational metrics and policy levers are presented in one decision frame.
evidenceToCollect: Where planners get stuck when moving from KPI views to intervention choices.
Which scenarios and views feel credible enough for cross-department use.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can deliver a credible planning layer if integration and KPI-pack standardization are reused rather than rebuilt per city.
evidenceToCollect: Which source systems are reusable across cities and which remain bespoke.
How much manual modeling work is required to stand up the first useful scenarios.

plannedActivities: Interview city policy and parking leaders in existing municipal accounts.
Replay recent pricing and curb-policy decisions to identify missing evidence.
Prototype a shared city intervention scorecard spanning parking and transport metrics.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to scale city intelligence as a distinct growth line or keep it as an attach module to operational accounts.
